title: If 3010 had a subtitle that followed the You Not naming convention instead of TUAT what would the subtitle be
display_title: If 3010 had a subtitle that followed the You Not naming convention instead of TUAT what would the subtitle be
wikitext:
[]: If 3.0+1.0 had a subtitle that followed the You - (Not) - naming convention instead of TUAT, what would the subtitle be?             Quote Post by FryMeTuhDehMuhn » Thu May 27, 2021 12:17 pm I think it could have been You Can (Not) Move On, as in the end that's what happens - They leave behind all Evangelions in their lives and move on. What do y'all think?

[]: Re: If 3.0+1.0 had a subtitle that followed the You - (Not) - naming convention instead of TUAT, what would the subtitle             Quote Post by Velorex » Thu May 27, 2021 2:39 pm FryMeTuhDehMuhn wrote: I think it could have been You Can (Not) Move On, as in the end that's what happens - They leave behind all Evangelions in their lives and move on. What do y'all think?

[]: Re: If 3.0+1.0 had a subtitle that followed the You - (Not) - naming convention instead of TUAT, what would the subtitle             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu May 27, 2021 3:01 pm Since the official numbering of TUAT is 3.0 + 1.0, I would go with "You Can Redo and You Are Alone", because that's basically Shinji's situation by the end of the movie. (well OK, there's Mari with him, but Shinji cut off the bridges with everyone else in his life)

[]: Re: If 3.0+1.0 had a subtitle that followed the You - (Not) - naming convention instead of TUAT, what would the subtitle             Quote Post by EvaChero » Thu May 27, 2021 3:53 pm This is [not] the end

[]: Re: If 3.0+1.0 had a subtitle that followed the You - (Not) - naming convention instead of TUAT, what would the subtitle             Quote Post by bogusman » Thu May 27, 2021 9:18 pm You can (not) say goodbye to Anno

[]: Re: If 3.0+1.0 had a subtitle that followed the You - (Not) - naming convention instead of TUAT, what would the subtitle             Quote Post by DantesInferno » Sat May 29, 2021 5:31 am You can (not) get over Shinji ending up with Mari (runs for cover…)

[]: Re: If 3.0+1.0 had a subtitle that followed the You - (Not) - naming convention instead of TUAT, what would the subtitle             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 29, 2021 9:38 am A lot of ppl were expecting "You will (not)survive" but that was under the assumption that it would be like EoE; In hindsight breaking the sequence seems like the right thing to do since it ended up being a franchise wide grand finale more than just "part 4 of 4" But if you wanted to find one, hmm... The others are pretty tied to the central emotion: The first one, like the series prologue arc, is about making a dent in Shinji's isolation. The biggest novel scene is the one where Misato gives him this speech about how the others are also risking their lives through the self-destruct mechanism. The second, both in-universe and for the makers, was a story about struggling with trying to change yourself for the better. The third is about coping with a grand sudden change & the realization that you can never go back.In the climax Shinji attempts to do something that he saw as possibly restoring the status quo or 'making up for' what happened (regardless of the details) & then it fails, forcing him to confront the irreversible entropy, as it were. Of course at the end of Q Shinji winds up thinking he has no allies left, that all he did amounted to making the same mistakes again and again, and that the world is completely lost - like we could sratch the 'not' in all three cases, basically. At the end of TuaT, it looks very different - he definitely has allies, and did move forward. Less sure about the 'redoing' - of course he can't go back to his life as it was before the catastrophe. That isn't really changed, though he comes to see that there is still good stuff left. On the other hand, the biosphere gets fixed, he reconciles with Misato and Asuka, and Rei II turns up alive after all. (not as much of an asspull if you consider that the people were not really dead, but only 'transformed' so that they could eventually become part of Gendo's Utopia) So, if we tried to find a similar aspect in the last one & follow the same arc, it would have to be tied to Shinji's arc - ​"You will (not) endure" maybe? Cause at the start he spends this long time being completely broken, unable to move on or recover after all he's been through, and then in the second half, he manages to pull himself together and act determined even though he's still hurting inside. Probably best exemplified by when he steps on the Wunder - as per Reichu's audio description project, still teary-eyed from what happened to ReiQ, but, he came anyway. Well. I suppose the "you can not move on" suggestion earlier expresses the same thought. its just niftier if its one word? Though "redo" is a bit clunky.
summary:
[]: If 3.0+1.0 had a subtitle that followed the You - (Not) - naming convention instead of TUAT, what would the subtitle be?             Quote Post by FryMeTuhDehMuhn » Thu May 27, 2021 12:17 pm I think it could have been You Can (Not) Move On, as in the end that's what happens - They leave behind all Evangelions in their lives and move on. What do y'all think?
